metadata_information.title: School effects on achievement in secondary mathematics and Portuguese in Brazil
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: School effects on achievement in secondary mathematics and Portuguese in Brazil

document_description.abstract: The authors use a multilevel modeling procedure to explore 1) the percentage of variance in secondary school achievement in Brazil that could be attributed to the types of school attended, 2) differences between schools in students' achievement in mathematics and Portuguese, and 3) differences between schools in reducing achievement differences based on students' socioeconomic status. The authors identified the following : students in Brazil's federal technical schools outperformed students in other schools in both mathematics and Portuguese. Important factors were class size (achievement was higher in larger classes), the number of hours math was taught (the more the better), the school's organizational complexity, average family social class background, and the number of hours students spent working.
